# Brainstorm (brz)

> Browser automation CLI for humans and LLM agents. Define workflows in YAML, execute them with `brz`, get structured JSON results. Designed for AI agents that need to automate browser tasks: inspect pages, fill forms, click buttons, download files.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/README.md): Full documentation, all commands, JSON schemas, workflow YAML spec, data pipeline patterns
- [Getting Started](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md): Installation, first workflow, running actions
- [Workflow Spec](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/docs/workflow-spec.md): Complete YAML schema for workflow files
- [Architecture](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/docs/architecture.md): How brz works, Go library API, package structure

## Commands

- `brz inspect <url>`: Discover interactive elements on a page (inputs, buttons, links). Returns CSS selectors usable in workflow YAML.
- `brz screenshot <url>`: Capture a full-page screenshot, returns file path as JSON.
- `brz eval <url> <js>`: Execute JavaScript on a page, returns result as JSON. Promises auto-awaited.
- `brz run <workflow.yaml> <action>`: Execute a named workflow action. Returns structured result with download paths.
- `brz validate <workflow.yaml>`: Check workflow YAML syntax.
- `brz actions <workflow.yaml>`: List available actions in a workflow.
- `brz prompt`: Print the full LLM agent prompt. Paste into your system prompt to teach any LLM how to use brz.

## Key Features

- JSON output by default when piped (for LLM agents, scripts, jq)
- Semantic exit codes: 0=success, 1=step failed, 2=workflow error, 3=browser error
- Persistent Chrome profile: login once, session survives across invocations
- Page discovery: inspect returns selectors an LLM can use to write workflow YAML
- No site-specific code: all automation defined in YAML
- Go library: importable as `github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/workflow`

## When to use brz

- You need to automate a website that has no API (browser automation is the only option)
- You want an LLM agent to discover page structure and generate automation workflows
- You need to download/upload files from web apps (CSV exports, file imports)
- You want structured JSON results from browser automation (not just "did it work")
- You need a single binary with no runtime dependencies (Go, not Node/Python)

## Examples

- [Form Login](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/workflows/examples/form-login.yaml)
- [CSV Export](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/workflows/examples/export-csv.yaml)
- [CSV Export and POST to API](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/workflows/examples/csv-export-and-post.yaml)
- [API POST JSON](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/workflows/examples/api-post-json.yaml)
- [Multi-step Upload](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/workflows/examples/multi-step-upload.yaml)
- [CAPTCHA Fallback](https://github.com/crackfetch/brainstorm/blob/main/workflows/examples/captcha-fallback.yaml)
